BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA

Stephanie Hickey

2024-12-10

Lab: Weiming Li

Project: BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA

Sample: UND2_C00004362

Detect empty droplets with DropletUtils

Empty droplets often contain RNA from the ambient solution, resulting in non-zero counts after debarcoding. The emptyDrops function is designed to distinguish between empty droplets and cells. It does so by testing each barcode’s expression profile for significant deviation from the ambient profile. Using this method, 18692 barcodes represent cells.

Below is a barcode rank plot, or knee plot, which shows the (log-)total UMI count for each barcode on the y-axis and the (log-)rank on the x-axis. The plot resembles a curve with a sharp “knee” or “elbow.” Cells with high RNA content (likely real biological cells), are on the left side of the plot. Low-RNA-content cells (likely empty droplets or low-quality cells) are on the right side. The “knee” or transition point marks the threshold between high-quality cells and low-quality or empty droplets.

Identifying low quality cells

Common metrics include for identifying low quality cells include:

  • Total counts
  • Number of expressed features (genes)
  • Proportion of reads mapped to genes in the mitochondrial genome

For each cell, we calculate these QC metrics using the perCellQCMetrics() function from the scater package.

Summarize the stats

The total counts in each cell:

##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    2067    4001    6407   11184   11530  406915

The number of genes with at least one count in each cell:

##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##     311    1028    1436    1752    2064   11851

These are the genes on the mitochondrial chromosome, NC_001626.1.

##  [1] "CYTB" "ND1"  "ND2"  "COX1" "COX2" "ATP8" "ATP6" "COX3" "ND3"  "ND4L"
## [11] "ND4"  "ND5"  "ND6"

The percent of counts that come from mitochondria genes:

##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.0000  0.8997  1.3531  1.6931  1.9655 22.6413

Normally, I would filter out cells with high percent mitochondria, but female germ cells likely have biologically high percent mitochondria. So I will cluster first and attempt to identify female germ cells before performing any QC filtering.

Cluster the cells and check for enrichment of known cell-type markers

The plot below is a UMAP dimentionality reduction plot where each cell is a dot colored by its cluster membership.

This table contains the top ~100 cluster enriched genes for each cluster after removing mitochondria genes. It is saved in UND2_C00004362_cluster_marker_genes.csv. The markers can help us map a cluster to a cell type.

Column descriptions

  • gene_id vector of gene names
  • cell_group character vector of the cell group to which the cell belongs
  • marker_score numeric vector of marker scores as the fraction expressing scaled by the specificity. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • mean_expression numeric vector of mean normalized expression of the gene in the cell group
  • fraction_expressing numeric vector of fraction of cells expressing the gene within the cell group
  • specificity numeric vector of a measure of how specific the gene’s expression is to the cell group based on the Jensen-Shannon divergence. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • pseudo_R2 numeric vector of pseudo R-squared values, a measure of how well the gene expression model fits the categorical data relative to the null model. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • marker_test_p_value numeric vector of likelihood ratio p-values
  • marker_test_q_value numeric vector of likelihood ratio q-values

We’ll check for the significance of the overlap between the top 100 cluster enriched genes and the supplied known cell-type markers. In the plot below, the rows represent cell types with know marker genes, and the columns represent the cell clusters. The number in each box is the number of genes shared between the top 100 cluster enriched genes for the specified clusters and the specified known cell-type markers. Each box is colored by the significance of the overlap (\(-log10(FDR)\)). Significant overlaps may signify that the cells in the cluster belong to the specified cell type. In this case, female germ cells may be represented by cluster 5 and/or 8.

A table with the overlapping genes is shown below. Filtering for Group1 = “Female Germ Cell” and Group2 = “8” will show the genes shared between those groups.

Plot percent mitochodria reads per cell

Shown here is a UMAP dimentionality reduction plot where each cell is a dot colored by the percent mitochondria genes detected in the cell.

Plot Mitochondria reads percent per cluster

Shown here is a violin plot showing the distribution of the mitochondria gene reads among cells in each cluster. The cells in Clusters 5 and 8, the presumed female germ cell cluster, do not have an unusually high percent of mitochondria reads. I’d like to filter cells for high mitochondria reads percentage to improve the dataset quality, if you agree that cluster 9 represents the female germ cells. I propose removing every cell above the red dotted line, percent mito > 3.6472871, which is 3 median absolute deviations (MAD) above the median percent mito. This is 1238 out of 18692 cells.

Shown here is a UMAP dimentionality reduction plot where each cell is a dot colored by whether or not the cell will be discarded.

Remove high mito cells and re-cluster

Check for enrichment of known cell-type markers

This table contains the top ~100 cluster enriched genes for each cluster after removing mitochondria genes. It is saved in UND2_C00004362_cluster_marker_genes_mito_filtered.csv. The markers can help us map a cluster to a cell type.

Column descriptions

  • gene_id vector of gene names
  • cell_group character vector of the cell group to which the cell belongs
  • marker_score numeric vector of marker scores as the fraction expressing scaled by the specificity. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • mean_expression numeric vector of mean normalized expression of the gene in the cell group
  • fraction_expressing numeric vector of fraction of cells expressing the gene within the cell group
  • specificity numeric vector of a measure of how specific the gene’s expression is to the cell group based on the Jensen-Shannon divergence. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • pseudo_R2 numeric vector of pseudo R-squared values, a measure of how well the gene expression model fits the categorical data relative to the null model. The value ranges from 0 to 1.
  • marker_test_p_value numeric vector of likelihood ratio p-values
  • marker_test_q_value numeric vector of likelihood ratio q-values

Again, we’ll check for the significance of the overlap between the top 100 cluster enriched genes and the supplied known cell-type markers. In the plot below, the rows represent cell types with know marker genes, and the columns represent the cell clusters. The number in each box is the number of genes shared between the top 100 cluster enriched genes for the specified clusters and the specified known cell-type markers. Each box is colored by the significance of the overlap (\(-log10(FDR)\)).

Now, cluster 8 is a likely female germ cell cluster. Although, there is weak evidence for any other cell-type classification with this method. Perhaps the results will be clearer when the four data sets are combined.

A table with the overlapping genes is shown below. Filtering for Group1 = “Female Germ Cell” and Group2 = “8” will show the genes shared between those groups.
